commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual review is the right lane because the PR has a protected maintainer label and a provider-routing merge-risk decision, while no automated code repair is indicated.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript migration plumbing and focused tests; it adds no dependencies, workflows, permissions, downloads, lifecycle hooks, or new secret-handling surface. (sha=02020a4098e499b6613ccfbf64ed1b8c6c3963cb)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes migration provider-option construction and apply plumbing to use the resolved provider id, with regression coverage for Codex-only options when raw `opts.provider` is omitted.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a high-confidence source reproduction path: call `runMigrationApply` with `providerId: "codex"`, Codex-only options, and omitted raw `opts.provider`; current `main` builds provider options from raw `opts.provider` only. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is the right lane because the PR has a protected `maintainer` label and a provider-routing merge-risk decision, while no automated code repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(migrate): use resolved provider for options
target.body:
## Summary
- build migration provider options from the resolved provider id
- pass the resolved provider id through migration apply preflight and apply contexts
- add provider-options coverage for defaulted Codex migrations with Codex-only options

Fixes #83285.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-specific migration options should be included when the migrate apply path has already resolved the provider as Codex, even if raw apply options do not carry `provider: "codex"`.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `02020a4098` on branch `tmp-pr-83323-proof`, with the local Codex CLI available as `codex-cli 0.130.0`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json`; `node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>`; `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
Evidence after fix:

```text
command=openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json
exit=0
stderr bytes=0
providerId=codex
summary={"total":1,"planned":1,"migrated":0,"skipped":0,"conflicts":0,"errors":0,"sensitive":0}
items=archive:config.toml:planned
warnings=1

{
  "providerId": "codex",
  "source": "/tmp/tmp.R8gIPM8A9P",
  "target": "/tmp/tmp.R06PR6nO84/home/.openclaw/workspace",
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 1,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 0
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "archive:config.toml",
      "kind": "archive",
      "action": "archive",
      "source": "/tmp/tmp.R8gIPM8A9P/config.toml",
      "message": "Codex config is archived for manual review; it is not activated automatically",
      "details": {
        "archiveRelativePath": "config.toml"
      },
      "status": "planned"
    }
  ]
}

command=node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>
scenario=providerId is codex, raw opts.provider is omitted, --yes apply path runs preflight plan and apply
{
  "providerId": "codex",
  "source": "/tmp/codex-proof",
  "summary": {
    "total": 0,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 0
  },
  "items": [],
  "reportDir": "/root/.openclaw/migration/codex/2026-05-19T01-28-04.767Z"
}
{
  "rawProviderPresent": false,
  "providerId": "codex",
  "calls": [
    {
      "phase": "plan",
      "providerOptions": {
        "verifyPluginApps": true,
        "configPatchMode": "return"
      }
    },
    {
      "phase": "apply",
      "providerOptions": {
        "verifyPluginApps": true,
        "configPatchMode": "return"
      }
    }
  ]
}

RUN  v4.1.6 /root/src/openclaw-branches/base

✓ commands  ../../src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts (2 tests)
✓ commands  ../../src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts (1 test)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

Observed result after fix: the real Codex migration provider dry-run succeeds against a temp Codex home and reports the expected Codex plan. The direct apply-path proof then exercises the defaulted-provider bug: `runMigrationApply` is called with `providerId: "codex"` while raw `opts.provider` is omitted, and both provider `plan` and provider `apply` receive `configPatchMode: "return"` plus `verifyPluginApps: true`.
What was not tested: a live migration against a private authenticated Codex account with installed app-backed plugins; the real CLI proof uses a redacted temp Codex home, and the exact apply-path behavior is proven with a source-checkout `runMigrationApply` command plus focused regression tests.

Before evidence from `origin/main`:

```text
src/commands/migrate/providers.ts before this patch checked only raw opts.provider:
  if (opts.provider === "codex" && opts.verifyPluginApps === true) { ... }
  if (opts.provider === "codex" && opts.configPatchMode) { ... }

src/commands/migrate/apply.ts called:
  providerOptions: buildMigrationProviderOptions(params.opts)

When the apply path had resolved providerId="codex" but opts.provider was undefined, Codex-only providerOptions were omitted.
```

## Root Cause
- Root cause: provider option construction used the raw optional CLI provider field rather than the provider id already resolved by the migrate command.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no focused coverage for defaulted Codex migrations carrying Codex-only options.
- Contributing context: `createMigrationPlan` requires `opts.provider`, but `runMigrationApply` receives `providerId` separately while `opts.provider` can remain undefined.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit test.
- Target tests or files: `src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts`, `src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Codex-only provider options are built from the resolved provider id, omitted for other providers, and forwarded into the apply path when raw `opts.provider` is absent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to provider option construction and apply-path plumbing.

## Validation
- `openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>`
- `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
